item.title: 🇮🇷 Explosions on Irans Qeshm Island from enemy ammunition disposal
item.description: Iranian officials report explosions on Qeshm Island were caused by neutralizing unexploded enemy ammunition, amid ongoing tensions in the Persian Gulf.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Four US LNG vessels sailing to China after Trump-Xi summit
item.description: First direct US LNG cargoes to China during Trump second term signal potential energy cooperation despite geopolitical tensions.

item.title: 🇪🇺 G7 economic agenda clouded by inflation fears as Iran war persists
item.description: US and Europe clash over Russan oil sanctions relief as energy shocks from Iran conflict drive inflation concerns at G7 meetings.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japans strong Q1 GDP faces Iran war reality check
item.description: Japans economy grew faster than expected in Q1, but energy shock from Iran conflict threatens momentum.

item.title: 🇨🇦 Canadas inflation jumps to 2.8% as Iran war drives gasoline prices higher
item.description: Annual inflation rate accelerated in April as oil price surge from Middle East conflict pushed gasoline costs up.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Russians Covertly Trained by China Return to Fight in Ukraine, Intelligence Sources Say
item.description: About 200 Russian military personnel were secretly trained by Chinese armed forces late last year and have returned to fight in Ukraine, according to European intelligence agencies.

item.title: 🇪🇪 NATO Fighter Jet Shoots Down Ukrainian Drone Over Estonia
item.description: A NATO fighter jet intercepted and shot down a Ukrainian drone that had entered Estonian airspace, highlighting the complexity of airspace management as Ukraine defends against Russian attacks.

item.title: 🇦🇪 UAE Says Drones That Hit Near Nuclear Plant Were Launched From Iraq
item.description: The UAE confirmed six drones were launched against it from Iraq in the past 48 hours, including one that caused a fire at the Barakah nuclear power plant, amid escalating regional tensions.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan Cautiously Optimistic on US Arms Sales Despite Trump Uncertainty
item.description: Taiwan Defense Minister Wellington Koo expressed cautious optimism about US arms sales as President Trump considers whether to proceed with new weapons deals to the self-governing island.

item.title: 🛡️ Pentagon awards M contract for counter-drone tech amid Iran war demand surge
item.description: The Defense Department awarded Perennial Autonomy a M contract to accelerate procurement of AI-enabled counter-drone systems including Merops interceptors and Bumblebee quadcopters, as the Iran conflict accelerates demand for defensive drone technology.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russian strikes damage 25 homes in Kharkiv, hit Izmail port infrastructure
item.description: Russia launched overnight strikes across Ukraine, injuring three people and damaging 25 homes in Kharkiv while hitting port infrastructure in Odesa's Izmail district. Rescue teams pulled two people from rubble as search operations continued.

item.title: 🏛️ Crimean Tatars face renewed persecution 82 years after Soviet deportation
item.description: On the anniversary of Stalin's 1944 deportation of 190,000 Crimean Tatars, activists describe Russia's ongoing persecution as "constant terror." Nearly 300 people from Crimea remain captive on politically motivated charges, including 159 Crimean Tatars.

item.title: 🇩🇪 Germany uncovers major sanctions evasion network supplying Russia via Turkey
item.description: German authorities uncovered a large sanctions-evasion network that routed European dual-use technology to Russia's military industry through Turkish intermediaries and shell companies. A 39-year-old businessman was arrested in raids linked to the four-year investigation.

item.title: 🦠 WHO convenes emergency meeting as rare Ebola strain kills 131 in Congo
item.description: The Bundibugyo virus, a rare Ebola species with no approved vaccines or treatments, has spread across eastern Congo with over 500 suspected cases. WHO Director-General expressed "deep concern" about the outbreak's scale and speed.

item.title: 🚢 Gaza aid flotilla organizers say 41 boats intercepted, 10 still sailing
item.description: Israeli forces raided dozens of vessels in international waters off Cyprus. Ten countries condemned the action as a "blatant violation of international law" and demanded release of detained activists including Irish President's sister.

item.title: 🌧️ At least 10 killed as heavy rains drench southern and central China
item.description: Torrential rain triggered widespread flooding across more than 1,000km of southern and central China, closing schools, businesses and disrupting transport. Chinese meteorologists warned of continued severe weather.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran submits peace proposal seeking war reparations and US troop withdrawal
item.description: Tehran has presented a peace proposal to the United States that includes ending hostilities across the region, withdrawal of US forces from areas near Iran, and reparations for war damage, as diplomatic efforts continue to de-escalate the conflict.

item.title: ☢️ Russia launches major nuclear forces drills amid Ukraine conflict escalation
item.description: Russia began three days of nuclear exercises involving 64,000 troops, over 200 missile launchers, and 140 aircraft. The drills come as Putin visits China and Ukraine steps up drone attacks on Russian territory.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukrainian medium-range drone strikes deal double blow to Russian war effort
item.description: Ukrainian mid-range drone attacks are increasingly targeting Russian oil refineries and disrupting ground offensives, presenting growing challenges to Moscow's military operations and logistics.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan premier warns China is greatest source of regional instability
item.description: Taiwan's Premier Cho Jung-tai stated that China's ongoing military activities near the island are the primary source of regional unease, as the defense ministry reports fresh Chinese military exercises nearby.

item.title: 🛢️ US Strategic Petroleum Reserve hits two-year low after record draw
item.description: A record 9.9 million barrels were withdrawn from the US emergency oil reserve last week, pushing total volumes to approximately 374 million barrels, the lowest level since July 2024, amid heightened energy market volatility.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran parliament to vote on €50 million bounty for Trump and Netanyahu
item.description: Iran’s National Security Council chairman announced a bill offering rewards for killing US President Trump, Israeli PM Netanyahu, and CENTCOM commander Cooper, calling it “reciprocal action” for the assassination of former Supreme Leader Khamenei.

item.title: 💥 Explosions heard on Qeshm Island in strategic Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Explosions were reported on Qeshm Island between the Persian Gulf and Strait of Hormuz, with air defenses activated amid heightened tensions following US warnings of possible strikes.

item.title: 🇮🇱 IDF destroys 60% of Hezbollah infrastructure in southern Lebanon
item.description: A senior IDF Northern Command officer said Israeli forces have destroyed 60% of Hezbollah terror infrastructure across 600 square kilometers in 63 villages within the Forward Defense Line zone.

item.title: 🇳🇬 Gunmen Abduct 39 Students and 7 Teachers in Nigeria School Attacks
item.description: Armed gunmen kidnapped 39 students and 7 teachers in coordinated attacks targeting a secondary school and two primary schools in Nigeria. Children aged between two and 16 were among those abducted in the latest mass kidnapping.
